name: Controlled Resource Teardown
description: Safely destroy Terraform-managed resources with targeted destroys, plan previews, and protection mechanisms.

steps.title: Preview what will be destroyed
steps.description: Generate a destroy plan to see exactly which resources will be removed before executing anything.
steps.tip: Always review the destroy plan. Terraform shows every resource that will be deleted.

steps.title: Save the destroy plan to a file
steps.description: Save the destroy plan so the exact reviewed changes are applied, nothing more or less.

steps.title: Destroy a specific resource only
steps.description: Target a single resource for destruction instead of tearing down everything.
steps.warning: Targeted destroys can leave orphaned dependent resources. Always check for dependencies first.

steps.title: Protect critical resources with lifecycle rules
steps.description: Add prevent_destroy to resources that should never be accidentally deleted.
steps.tip: With prevent_destroy = true, Terraform will error if any plan attempts to delete this resource.

steps.title: Execute the destroy plan
steps.description: Apply the saved destroy plan after thorough review and team confirmation.
steps.warning: This is irreversible for most resources. Ensure backups exist for databases and persistent storage before proceeding.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

# Safe Terraform Destroy Workflow

TARGET="${1:-}"

echo "==> Current resources in state:"
terraform state list

if [ -n "$TARGET" ]; then
  echo ""
  echo "==> Planning targeted destroy: ${TARGET}"
  terraform plan -destroy -target="$TARGET" -out=destroy.tfplan
else
  echo ""
  echo "==> Planning FULL destroy..."
  terraform plan -destroy -out=destroy.tfplan
fi

echo ""
RESOURCE_COUNT=$(terraform show -no-color destroy.tfplan | grep -c "will be destroyed" || echo "0")
echo "Resources to destroy: ${RESOURCE_COUNT}"

echo ""
read -p "Type 'destroy' to confirm: " CONFIRM
if [ "$CONFIRM" = "destroy" ]; then
  echo "==> Executing destroy..."
  terraform apply destroy.tfplan
  echo "==> Destroy complete."
else
  echo "==> Destroy cancelled."
  rm -f destroy.tfplan
fi

faq.question: Can I undo a terraform destroy?
faq.answer: No. Destroyed resources are deleted from both the cloud provider and Terraform state. You would need to recreate them with terraform apply or restore from backups.

faq.question: How do I remove a resource from Terraform without destroying it?
faq.answer: Use 'terraform state rm <resource_address>' to remove it from state without deleting the actual cloud resource. The resource continues to exist but is no longer managed by Terraform.
